name: Bennie Maupin
abstract: Bennie Maupin (born August 29, 1940) is an American jazz multi-instrumentalist who performs on various saxophones, flute, and bass clarinet.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Donald Byrd and The Blackbyrds, and Herbie Hancock Septet

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Get Stanley Cowell on the piano and Benny Maupin on the saxophone... That was my first record date... That had Benny Maupin on bass clarinet, tenor, voice... It was great to play with Gary and Benny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: He had a sextet that could play straight ahead, out, all kinds of stuff: Freddie Waits, Bennie Maupin, James Spaulding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: So, we used Benny Maupin... I used that rhythm section... We recorded an album called “You Had Better Listen” in 1967.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: the great Bennie Maupin on tenor saxophone. So I couldn’t have asked for more — and the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Bitches Brew was basically kind of a jam session with ... Bennie Maupin would play, different players would solo on top of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Mark Soskin
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve used Bennie Maupin and he’s great. I’m still in touch with Bennie... we’ve talked about things, actually doing things again
about_quotes.doc_title: Mark Soskin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 17, 2021
